figures.caption: Table 1: Speech enhancement results on the VoiceBank-DEMAND test set (mean scores). Best values are in bold, second-best are underlined. † indicates that the score model was trained on outputs from NCSN++, thereby constituting a mismatch with the predictor used during inference.
Figure 3: Speech enhancement performance over κ on the matched and mismatched validation sets.
Table 5: Speech enhancement results on the VoiceBank-DEMAND test set (mean scores)
Figure 6: Speech enhancement performance over number of steps N on the matched and mismatched validation sets.

Figure 7: Schematic of the proposed framework. The predictor Pϕ defines a deterministic drift v̂ = Pϕ(y)−y from the observation y, which steers the sampling dynamics at every step. Concurrently, the denoiser Dθ guides the trajectory toward regions of higher likelihood. After the sampling procedure, the predictor is optionally applied again as a post-processing step.

Table 2: Mismatched scenario: Speech enhancement results on the EARS-WHAM (v2) test set (mean scores). Best values are in bold, second-best are underlined. † indicates that the score model was trained on outputs from NCSN++, thereby constituting a mismatch with the predictor used during inference.
Table 7: Speech enhancement results on the VoiceBank-DEMAND test set.
Table 6: Mismatched scenario: Speech enhancement results on the EARS-WHAM (v2) test set.
Table 4: Speech enhancement results on VoiceBank-DEMAND for different noise schedule hyperparameters a and c.
Table 3: Speech separation results on WHAMR!. For the mixture, SI-SDR and PESQ are computed with respect to each speaker’s utterance and averaged.

Figure 4: SI-SDR and UTMOS on WHAMR! for FlexIO with and without the generative prior. The diagonal line indicates equal performance. Individual test utterances are shown as points, with dense regions visualized as a density cloud.
Table 8: Speech enhancement results on the VoiceBank-DEMAND test set (mean ± standard deviation). Best values are in bold and second-best are underlined.
